methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The Genesis contract must always hold enough Virtual tokens to cover all unrefunded user commitments, accounting for the reserve amount if the agent has launched.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract balance falls below the committed amounts (e.g., due to admin withdrawing too much via withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized), users will be unable to claim their refunds, leading to a loss of funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_virtualTokenSolvency() public view { uint256 expected = 0; for(uint i=0; i<participants.length; i++) { expected += mapAddrToVirtuals[participants[i]]; } if (agentTokenAddress != address(0)) { expected -= reserveAmount; } assert(IERC20(virtualTokenAddress).balanceOf(address(this)) >= expected); }

invariants.description: The Genesis contract must hold enough Agent tokens to satisfy all outstanding claimable balances for users.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract lacks sufficient Agent tokens, users who were allocated tokens during a successful genesis cannot claim them.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_agentTokenSolvency() public view { uint256 expected = 0; for(uint i=0; i<participants.length; i++) { expected += claimableAgentTokens[participants[i]]; } assert(IERC20(agentTokenAddress).balanceOf(address(this)) >= expected); }

invariants.description: The amount of tax swapped for an agent can never exceed the total amount of tax collected for that agent.
invariants.attack_scenario: If swapped exceeds collected, the contract would be using tax tokens belonging to other agents, causing insolvency for those agents' token bound accounts.

invariants.description: The sum of the protocol fee rate and the creator fee rate must always equal exactly 100% (DENOM = 10000) to ensure all swapped assets are fully distributed.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the sum is less than 10000, swapped assets will be permanently trapped in the contract. If greater, the transaction will revert or drain other assets.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_feeRateConsistency() public view { assert(feeRate + creatorFeeRate == 10000); }

invariants.description: Once a token graduates to Uniswap (tradingOnUniswap becomes true), its bonding curve trading state must be permanently disabled.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a token could revert to trading == true after graduation, attackers could interact with a depleted bonding curve pair, manipulating prices or extracting trapped liquidity.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_graduationMonotonicity(address token) public view { if (tokenInfo[token].tradingOnUniswap) { assert(!tokenInfo[token].trading); } }

invariants.description: The value of k in the FPair contract must remain strictly constant after the initial mint. It defines the invariant of the bonding curve.
invariants.attack_scenario: If k could be modified during swaps, the pricing curve would shift, allowing attackers to manipulate the price and drain the bonding curve.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_kIsConstant() public view { assert(_pool.k == initial_k); }
